Off Calendar Feb-27-2017 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-30-2016 Granted Per Written Order

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (743-745 Capp St., San Francisco, Ca)
Off Calendar Jul-22-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Rasch for (administrator) Debra Dolch.

The Court stated that there is a procedural issue as to whether there is proof of publication of the notice. Ms. Rasch responded that the will authorizes the sale and that they are not required to publish. The Court stated that it is not entirely clear to the Court that it excuses notice. Ms. Rasch stated that they have never published notice when the will authorizes sale. The Court stated that it will proceed with the sale subject to the Court satisfying its question on publication of notice. The sale was confirmed at $1,511,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed 6/28/16 and subject to the existing tenants rights. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order and clarification of the procedural issue). An additional bond in the amount to be determined by counsel is required. Ms. Rasch clarified that the will directs the sale of property. Mr. Coleman (realtor) stated that the buyers are also the beneficiaries and did receive notice.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

Off Calendar Aug-11-2015 Continued To Aug-26-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jul-14-2015 Continued To Aug-11-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Chris Dewys for and with (petitioner) Ruben Herrera. Rowena Navia for and with Abel Herrera. Dolores Gonzalez in Propria Persona. Alberto Herrera also present.

The court stated that there are 3 competing petitions for Probate and asked if the parties have talked about ways to move forward. Mr. Dewys reported that the parties spoke after the last hearing, that they are at an impasse and that there have been informal attempts at a solution but they have been unable to come to one. Ms. Navia stated that they have discussed using a private professional and that her client is agreeable to that if the others agree. Ms. Gonzalez stated that she has no issue with Ruben or any of the other siblings with the exception of Abel being appointed and stated that her petition should be considered as she is the best qualified. The court stated that her petition is not properly before it, that the will appoints both her and Abel and that absent an agreement to a private professional it is not willing to appoint only one of you. Ms. Gonzalez stated that the procedural defects in her petition can be corrected, that she has been trusted with the assets since she was 19 years old, that she is a member of the bar, that she would faithfully execute her duties and that she has no objection to being bonded. Mr. Dewys stated that the preference is either Dolores only or a 3rd party. Ms. Navia stated that there are 2 choices, the first is a private professional fiduciary and the second is the appointment of co-executors pursuant to the will and that the other petitions have no authority. The court stated that a private fiduciary needs to be appointed, that it will give the parties the opportunity to agree on someone, that it will continue the 3 current petitions with the expectation that a new petition or an amended petition will be filed, that if by August 4, 2015 there is no agreement on an individual each side is to submit 3 names and the court will decide and that this submission is to be filed no later than August 5, 2015 and will include names, professional histories and the willingness of the individual to serve. The 3 petitions for probate of will and for letters testamentary were continued to August 11, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jul-07-2015 Continued To Jul-14-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jun-09-2015 Continued To Jul-14-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Rowena Navia for and with (petitioner) Abel Herrera. Chris Dewys for and with (petitioner) Ruben Herrera. Dolores Gonzales (petitioner) in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that there is now a petition filed by Dolores Gonzales with a hearing date of July 7, 2015 and that the two petitions on today will not go forward until then, that the parties need to agree on a professional fiduciary and that absent a professional fiduciary will need to give reason why the court should not go with the decedents wishes and reminded the parties that there will not be much left if they continue to fight over it. Ms. Navia requested a date further out than 7/7/15 and stated that they are open to mediation. Mr. Dewys stated that they would like to explore matters informally first. The two petitions for probate of will and for letters testamentary were continued to July 14, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The court further ordered that the petition filed by Ms. Gonzales be continued from July 7, 2015 to that date as well.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jun-09-2015 Continued To Jul-14-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar May-12-2015 Continued To Jun-09-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Christopher Dewys for and with (Petitioner) Ruben Herrera. Rowena Navia for and with (Beneficiary & Co-Executor) Abel Herrera.

Ms. Navia reported that they attempted to meet and confer and that Mr. Dewys requested that the hearing go forward without meeting. Mr. Dewys cited CRC 7.801, stated that the court has the ability to waive the objection filed yesterday, that it was untimely and that Mr. Herrera had been instructed at 3 previous hearings to file his objections. The Court stated that it would not be waiving the objections and that now that everyone has counsel maybe the family can figure things out. Mr. Dewys stated that document is not really an objection and that it asks for an accounting prior to appointment. Ms. Navia stated that what is filed is an objection, that she would like time to meet and confer with Ruben Herrera and his counsel and to explore the possibility of a neutral private fiduciary. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary was continued to June 9, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the parties to talk about how to proceed.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Lopes. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Apr-14-2015 Continued To May-12-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ruben Herrera (petitioner) in Propria Persona. Abel Herrera (co-executor) in Propria Persona. Alberto Herrera & Dolores Gonzalez (heirs) also present.

Ms. Gonzalez reported that they have a letter & fee agreement from attorney Chris De Wys of the Danforo Law Offices who will be representing Ruben Herrera. Mr. Abel Herrera stated that he was objecting but if Ruben is the only petitioner he will not object and expressed concern and confusion regarding the authority that Ruben would have. The court stated (again) that Mr. Abel Herrera needs an attorney if he is unsure of the procedures and that if he does not file an objection the court will treat the matter as if there is no objection, without prejudice to further petitions filed. The court also stated that a substitution of attorney has not been filed and that the court will continue this petition 1 more time for the attorney to make an appearance and for Mr. Abel Herrera to either file or forego his objection. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary was continued to May 12, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Feb-24-2015 Continued To Apr-14-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ruben Herrera (petitioner) in Propria Persona. Abel Herrera (co-executor) in Propria Persona. Dolores Gonzalez and Alberto Herrera (heirs) also present.

The court asked Mr. Abel Herrera why he did not file his objection as he was specifically told to do so in advance of this date to give the other parties time to respond. Ms. Gonzalez reported that they have gotten anywhere since the last date, they have contacted an attorney, Chris De Wiss who will provide limited scope representation after they get past this point. Mr. Abel Herrera stated that he has consulted an attorney but not retained one as of yet. The court stated that it may be appropriate to have an independent administrator to complete what was requested last time, that it may help keep the family together. The court also stated that if there are any objections they must be in writing and filed well in advance of continuance date, that it is important to get legal advice on working out who will be the administrator or decide on independent administrator which would require a petition and notice in time for the next hearing. The petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary was continued to April 14, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Murillo. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jan-20-2015 Continued To Feb-24-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ruben Herrera (petitioner) in Propria Persona. Abel Herrera (stated co-executor) in Propria Persona. Dolores Gonzalez & Alberto Herrera also present.

The court stated we are here on Ruben Herrera's petition, asked Mr. Abel Herrera if he planned to file his own petition and asked if anyone else objected to Ruben's petition, indicating it would continue this petition and instructed everyone to submit objections in writing, serve all parties and Ruben would have time to respond. The court also stated that in light of the complications of this case it suggests attorney representation. Abel Herrera requested to be sent to mediation, the court stated that attorneys would be a help in this regard. The petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary was continued to February 24, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to allow objections and or competing petitions to be filed.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Loos. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Nov-12-2014 Continued To Dec-10-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dolores Gonzalez in Propria Persona. Abel Herrera in Propria Persona.

Ms. Gonzalez stated that she received the Probate Examiner's notes and that she answered the questions/filed an amended petition on Friday. Mr. Herrera stated that he received some legal opinions, that there are problems with the family, that there are substantial bank accounts and raised the issue of undue influence. Ms. Gonzalez presented oral argument to support having one person (herself) appointed. The Court raised the issue of having a professional appointed and stated that the Parties need to have an attorney. The Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary was continued to December 10, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the Parties to discuss the matter.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Judge: Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy L. Maloney. (Not Reported)
